context_note: Netherlands vs Japan - a genuinely competitive group-stage match, unlike the lopsided GER-CUR. Smarkets has Netherlands ~46.6% / Draw ~27.9% / Japan ~25.6%, a real three-way race.

smarkets.method: mid-price = avg(best_bid, best_offer) per contract (1/100ths of a percent), vig-removed via normalization across all contracts in a market

sanity_check_full_match_sot_lines.note: used as market-anchor blend for Q1 (2H SOT, no direct 2H market exists)

japan_n3.note: All 5 fifa.worldq.afc events (2024-2025) returned empty boxscore.statistics, same gap as Curacao in GER-CUR - excluded. Only the 3 most recent 2026 friendlies usable.

q1_both_teams_1plus_sot_2h.method: No direct 2H-SOT market exists. Built Skellam-style independent-probability product: P(team scores 1+ SOT in 2H) = 1-e^-lambda_2H, lambda_2H = full-match-SOT-mean/2. Full-match means BLENDED between ESPN recent-form and Smarkets full-match O/U SOT lines (the QAT-SUI Q3 winning pattern, +19.86): Japan ESPN n=3 avg=5.333 vs market-implied ~3.65 (large gap - Japan's 2026 friendly SOT numbers, esp. vs Iceland/Scotland, likely overstate output vs a much stronger Netherlands defense) blended 70/30 toward market = 4.155 -> 2H lambda=2.08 -> P=0.875. Netherlands ESPN n=8 avg=5.125 vs market ~4.7, close already, blended 50/50 = 4.91 -> 2H lambda=2.46 -> P=0.914. Product = 0.875*0.914.

q2_japan_more_fouls_than_netherlands.method: Skellam normal approx on per-match foul rates: Japan n=3 avg=13.333, Netherlands n=8 avg=9.875, mean_diff=3.458, var=sum of means (Poisson)=23.21, z=0.718, raw P=0.764. SHRUNK toward 0.5 to 0.70 to account for Japan's thin n=3 sample (regression-to-mean shrinkage, not a narrative shade). Direction is reinforced, not contradicted, by the underdog-fouls-more pattern (QAT-SUI Q6, GER-CUR Q1): Japan as the weaker side defending against Netherlands should commit MORE fouls than its n=3 average suggests, not fewer - so shrinking toward 0.5 (rather than further toward 1.0) is the conservative choice given no market exists to anchor against.

q3_netherlands_2plus_offsides.method: First-pass Poisson, Netherlands offsides n=8, lambda=1.5, P(X>=2)=1-e^-lambda(1+lambda)=0.4422. Identical structure and nearly identical value to GER-CUR Q5 (Germany 2+ offsides, also lambda=1.5, 0.4422) and BRA-MAR Q1 (Brazil 2+ offsides, 0.43) - both unshaded first-pass numbers, both wins (+20.36 for BRA-MAR Q1). No market exists for offsides. Submitted unshaded per the established 'first-pass wins' pattern.

q4_btts_and_3plus_total_goals.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Both teams to score and over/under 2.5' market, 'Yes/Over' contract = BTTS AND >2.5 goals = BTTS AND >=3 total goals, exact match for this question. Vig-adjusted 38.74%. Undeviated per RULE2.

q5_japan_more_cards_than_netherlands.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Most cards' market, Japan outcome = Japan has strictly more cards than Netherlands (draw = equal), vig-adjusted 44.19%. Undeviated - our most consistent category (+5.00 avg, 85% correct side over 13 questions).

q6_netherlands_win.method: RULE1 (avg(crowd,market)) cannot be computed pre-match (crowd unobservable). Unlike GER-CUR's lopsided 94.6% favorite, this is a genuinely competitive three-way (NED 46.6% / Draw 27.9% / JPN 25.6%) - well within normal range, no RULE6 capping needed. Smarkets full-time-result market, vig-adjusted, treated as tier1_market undeviated (our most proven category) rather than a 'gut' number.

q7_japan_scores_in_2h.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Second half Japan over/under 0.5 goals' market, Over=Japan scores 1+ in 2H, vig-adjusted 44.96%. Undeviated.

q8_8plus_total_sot.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Over/under 7.5 shots on target' (combined total) market, Over=8+, vig-adjusted 59.21%. Undeviated.

q9_gakpo_1plus_sot.method: Direct from Smarkets player-SOT market, Cody Gakpo 'over 0.5 shots on target' = 1+ SOT, two-sided liquid quote, mid-price 60.86%. Undeviated.

q10_kubo_score_or_assist.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Player to score or assist' market, Takefusa Kubo, offer-only (32.26%, no bid - illiquid), same situation as GER-CUR Q10 (Bacuna). Submitted slightly below the one-sided offer using the same ratio (0.945) applied there: 0.3226*0.945=0.305.

fouls_in_order: 90+1' NED (van de Ven, YELLOW - 2-3min after conceding 88' equalizer)
goal_and_foul_timeline.running_foul_tally_at_88min_japan_goal: JPN 6 - NED 5 (Japan AHEAD on fouls when they scored the late equalizer)

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Japan commit more fouls than Netherlands?
questions.note: OUR WORST RESULT THIS MATCH. Japan was AHEAD on fouls 6-5 at the moment of their 88' equalizer - this question was on track to resolve YES. Netherlands' very last foul of the match (90+1', van de Ven, carded for 'a bad foul' - an emotional/reactive tackle thrown in the immediate aftermath of conceding the dramatic late equalizer) tied the count 7-7, flipping the outcome to NO. A single late reactive foul, itself CAUSED by the same event (Japan's 88' goal) that pushed Q7 to YES, flipped this question against us.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Netherlands be caught offside 2 or more times?
Will both teams have at least 1 shot on target in the second half?
Will both teams score AND the match have 3 or more total goals?
Will Japan receive more cards than Netherlands?
Will Japan score in the second half?
questions.note: Japan scored TWICE in the 2H (57', 88'). The 88' goal is the SAME event that flipped Q1 against us - directly correlated loss.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will there be 8 or more total shots on target?
Will Cody Gakpo have at least 1 shot on target?
Will Takefusa Kubo score or assist a goal (excluding own goals)?

post_match_results.summary: Near-perfect wash (+0.47) masking a single correlated failure mode: Q1 (Japan fouls more, -25.37, our worst result) and Q7 (Japan scores in 2H, -2.95) were BOTH driven by the SAME event - Japan's dramatic 88th-minute equalizer (2-2 final). Japan was actually AHEAD on fouls 6-5 heading into that minute (Q1 was on track for a big WIN); Netherlands' immediate emotional response - a reckless tackle by van de Ven 2-3 minutes later, carded 'for a bad foul' - tied the foul count 7-7 and flipped Q1 to a loss. Both teams ended up UNDER their historical foul averages (JPN avg 13.33 -> actual 7; NED avg 9.875 -> actual 7), converging to an exact tie - in a genuinely competitive match (NED 46.6%/Draw 27.9%/JPN 25.6% pre-match), counting-stat 'more than' props are inherently tie-prone, which our historical-average Skellam model didn't account for. New candidate RULE8: for 'Team A more [count stat] than Team B' questions, high pre-match draw probability should shrink the estimate further toward (or below) 50% than team-level historical averages alone suggest, because evenly-matched teams' counting stats are more likely to converge/tie (tie=NO).
